1. `20_Project/Japan_Itinerary/UTokyo_RIKEN.md`
2. `90_Plans/Plan_2026-02-15_UTokyo_Oct2027_Application.md`

Specifically verify:
- All references to JRA salary say 250k (not 200k) except in historical/archival context
- All references to commuting say 150k (not 55k)
- V2 and V3 in the verification tracker say VERIFIED with correct sources
- Net income estimates are consistent with 250k gross (~200–210k net)
- The tuition exemption note references 3.0M (not 2.4M) for JRA annual income
- V12 reference updated from 200k to 250k
- Line 196 in the execution plan updated
- Line 44 in the execution plan updated
- Line 61 in the execution plan marked as done

Report any inconsistencies found. Do NOT make edits — just report.

   200→### Route A — Regular Physics Exam (一般入試)
   201→

   203→> **Format:** 240 min, 4 mandatory problems (全問必答), 400 points total
   204→> | Problem | Subject | Weight |
   205→> |---------|---------|--------|
   206→> | 1 | Mathematics (linear algebra, complex analysis, Fourier, ODEs) | 100 pts |
   207→> | 2 | Quantum Mechanics | 100 pts |

   209→> | 4 | Classical Mechanics + Electromagnetism | 100 pts |

   219→
   220→### Route B — GSGC Physics (Global Science Graduate Course)
   221→
   222→> [!info] Nuclear Physics availability (theory + experiment)
   223→> - Dept. of Physics sub-courses include **Nuclear Theory (A0)** and **Experimental Nuclear/Particle Physics (A2)**.
   224→> - The Dept. of Physics participates in GSGC and all graduate courses are conducted in English → nuclear theory/experimental nuclear/particle are within the GSGC scope (confirm specific supervisors via the GSGC faculty list).
   225→

   227→> - Selection based on application documents + test scores (GSGC guidelines for master's-level admission, updated 2025-09-01).
   228→> - **GRE Physics Subject Test required** for Physics; **GRE General not required**. TOEFL required (unless exemption).
   229→> - Some departments may add **interviews** and/or **online written examinations** (see department-specific notes).
   230→
   231→> [!warning] GSGC Physics written exam (official 2026 notice)
   232→> - Applicants must contact their prospective supervisor; if the supervisor deems it necessary, the applicant must take an **online written exam** (not all applicants take it).
   233→> - 2026 exam: **Tue Jan 20, 2026 (15:00–17:00 JST)**, 2 hours, **English**. Subjects: CM, QM, EM, Thermodynamics, StatMech.
   234→> - **Implication:** GSGC Physics is **not purely GRE-based**; there is a department-written exam when required. Official pages do not link any past papers for this exam; prioritize GRE Physics + core subjects and ask the supervisor/office if any past questions can be shared.
